rag_rules: Place input envelope assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds from the requirement.
Do not generate implementation-level guarantees or assign statements unless explicitly tied to outputs or auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees must reference only visible, typed symbols from the AADL model—never AADL properties or implicit scopes.
Use boolean expressions for range checks only when the underlying data is numeric and explicitly represented in scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use syntax: annex agree {** ... **}; and must be enclosed in double asterisks.
All expressions in AGREE contracts must use types consistent with AADL ports: bool, int, or real—never mixed or implicit conversions.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) or unit tokens (ms, Hz) as if they are AGREE variables.
Avoid generating range comparisons over Boolean or opaque ports without explicit numeric representation in the model.
irrelevant_topics: assign statement usage in implementation scope
AGREE annex fusion or traceability mapping
